phrasal_verbs.meaning: stop doing sth you do regularly
make a player leave a game (eg, football)

A.title: Complete the crossword
A.instructions: Solve the crossword using the clues below.

across.clue: The school football ...... meets every Wednesday after class. (4)
Our team needs to ...... two goals to win the match. (5)
The dance class listened carefully to the ...... of the music. (6)
We entered a swimming ...... at the sports centre. (11)
Liam wants to ...... for the race every morning. (5)

down.clue: Our ...... chose the players for Saturday's match. (7)
You must never ...... in a game or competition. (5)
The chess ...... shook my hand before the game. (8)
I get a lot of ...... from playing music with friends. (8)
The ...... stopped the match after the foul. (7)

B.title: Join our games club! (gap fill)
B.instructions: Complete using the correct form of the words and phrases in the box.
B.passage: Do you enjoy (1) ...... your friends at board games, or do you prefer trying to (2) ...... points in video games? Games (3) ...... lots of young people because they are exciting and social. At our club, you can learn new skills and (4) ...... with people your age. Every month, we (5) ...... a small competition for members. It can be a real (6) ...... to win, but our older players will help you (7) ...... and improve. Come along on Friday and try it!

questions.sentence: My brother spends hours playing **video / folk** games online.
The **referee / rhythm** gave two players a warning during the match.
We saw our favourite band at a huge **concert / coach** last night.
The tennis player accepted the **challenge / pleasure** and played again.
Our school **team / member** won the final by three points.
I prefer **classical / captain** music when I’m studying.

D.title: Phrasal verbs (type-in)
D.instructions: Complete each sentence with the correct particle.

questions.sentence: Maya wants to take ...... (up / off / out) dancing next term.
Please turn the TV ...... (down / in / off) because I'm trying to read.
Everyone was singing, so I decided to join ...... (up / in / out).
The referee sent the goalkeeper ...... (off / up / down) after a dangerous foul.
I love this part of the song! Turn it ...... (up / in / out)!
The lights went out, but the band carried ...... (on / off / up) playing.
We usually eat ...... (out / up / off) after football practice on Fridays.
He gave ...... (up / in / down) tennis when he hurt his knee.

E.title: Prepositional phrases (gap fill)
E.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

questions.sentence: We practised the song ...... a long time before the concert.
I've got their latest performance ...... DVD.
We arrived just ...... time for the start of the match.
The actors walked ...... stage and everyone clapped.
There was a small table ...... the middle of the room for the board game.
I don't want to win prizes. I only play chess ...... fun.

F.title: Word formation
F.instructions: Complete by changing the form of the word in capitals when this is necessary.

questions.sentence: The ...... gave an amazing performance at the concert. **SING**
My sister is very ...... and can play three instruments. **MUSIC**
Jake is strong, fast and very ...... . **ATHLETE**
Many ...... enjoy playing games outside after school. **CHILD**
The film was full of exciting ...... scenes. **ACT**
My cousin is a serious stamp ...... . **COLLECT**
We went ...... on the lake during our holiday. **SAIL**
That football ...... scored three goals in the final. **PLAY**

G.title: Young musicians (word formation)
G.instructions: Use the word given in capitals at the end of each line to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.
G.passage: Many children enjoy (1) ...... (SING) before they can read music. Some become excellent (2) ...... (MUSIC) when they are older. Learning an instrument is not always easy, but it is great (3) ...... (ENTERTAIN) for the whole family. Regular practice is an important (4) ...... (ACT), and young performers need to stay (5) ...... (ACT) if they want to improve. A confident child may even feel like a (6) ...... (HERO) when they perform on stage.

questions.sentence: (1) (SING)
(2) (MUSIC)
(3) (ENTERTAIN)
(6) (HERO)

H.title: Word patterns (dialogue)
H.instructions: Write one word in each gap.
H.passage:
Leo: Hi, Mia. Are you still crazy (1) ...... basketball?
Mia: Yes! I'm really keen (2) ...... it. We have a game (3) ...... a team from another school tomorrow.
Leo: That sounds exciting. Are you good (4) ...... shooting?
Mia: I'm getting better. Our coach says I should listen (5) ...... his advice and practise every day.
Leo: I'd like to take part (6) ...... a sport too, but I'm more interested (7) ...... music at the moment.

I.title: Word patterns (error correction)
I.instructions: Each of the words in bold is wrong. Write the correct word.

questions.sentence: I'm really interested **on** joining the drama club.
My brother is very good **in** table tennis.
We listened **at** classical music all evening.
Our team has got a game **with** City School on Saturday.
I'm a big fan **for** that singer.
Do you feel **for** playing a board game later?
